Now I see the full state:
- Node 7: color=G (dark gray, not charcoal 'c'), boundary=[36,32]-[39,35], children=[8] - this is the center-bottom frame
- Node 8: color=b (blue), boundary=[37,33]-[38,34] - blue target inside that frame
- Player (node 10): color=N (light green), boundary=[41,32]-[43,35]
- Marker (node 9): color=W (white), boundary=[40,32]-[40,35]
So the center-bottom frame is dark gray (G), not charcoal (c). The three charcoal frames are:
1. [24,44]-[27,47] with blue [25,45]-[26,46]
2. [28,16]-[31,19] with blue [29,17]-[30,18]
3. The larger one [28,28]-[31,39] with gray bar [29,29]-[30,38]
Wait, node 4 has color=b (blue), not c. Let me re-check... Node 4: color=b, pixels=28, boundary=[28,28]-[31,39]. That's the larger frame but it's blue, not charcoal. And node 6 inside it is gray (g).
So the frames are:
- 3 charcoal (c) frames: nodes 1, 3, and... wait, node 7 is dark gray (G), not charcoal. Let me recount.
  - Node 1: c, [24,44]-[27,47], child blue node 2
  - Node 3: c, [28,16]-[31,19], child blue node 5
  - Node 7: G, [36,32]-[39,35], child blue node 8 (this is dark gray, different color)
- 1 blue (b) frame: node 4, [28,28]-[31,39], child gray node 6
The player is at [41,32]-[43,35] and the dark gray frame with blue target is at [36,32]-[39,35]. The player is directly below it. Moving UP would bring the player to overlap with the frame.
Let me try one more UP to enter the frame.
